title: Charmed Life - Chapter 6 (Fragment 3)
text:
‚ÄúEr‚ÄĒmay I go down and have some tea?‚ÄĚ he stammered.

‚ÄúPlease yourself,‚ÄĚ she said‚ÄĒrather coldly, Cat thought. He ran downstairs again, feeling he might have gotten off on the wrong foot with her.

Gwendolen‚Äôs trunk was standing in the middle of her room. Gwendolen herself was sitting in a very queenly way at a round table by the window, with a big pewter teapot in front of her, a plate of brown bread and butter, and a plate of biscuits.

‚ÄúI told the girl I‚Äôd unpack for myself,‚ÄĚ she said. ‚ÄúI‚Äôve got secrets in my trunk and my box. And I asked her to bring tea at once because I‚Äôm starving. And just look at it! Did you ever see anything so dull? Not even jam!‚ÄĚ

‚ÄúPerhaps the biscuits are nice,‚ÄĚ Cat said hopefully. But they were not, or not particularly.

‚ÄúWe shall starve, in the midst of luxury!‚ÄĚ sighed Gwendolen.

Her room was certainly luxurious. The wallpaper seemed to be made of blue velvet. The top and bottom of the bed was upholstered like a chair, in blue velvet with buttons in it, and the blue velvet bedspread matched it exactly. The chairs were painted gold. There was a dressing table fit for a princess, with little golden drawers, gold-backed brushes, and a long oval mirror surrounded by a gilded wreath. Gwendolen admitted that she liked the dressing table, though she was not so sure about the wardrobe, which had painted garlands and maypole dancers on it.

‚ÄúIt‚Äôs to hang clothes in, not to look at,‚ÄĚ she said. ‚ÄúIt distracts me. But the bathroom is lovely.‚ÄĚ

The bathroom was tiled with blue and white tiles, and the bath was sunk down into the tiled floor. Over it, draped like a baby‚Äôs cradle, were blue curtains for when you wanted a shower. The towels matched the tiles. Cat preferred his own bathroom, but that may have been because he had to spend rather a long time in Gwendolen‚Äôs. Gwendolen locked him in it while she unpacked. Through the hiss of the shower‚ÄĒGwendolen had only herself to blame that she found her bathroom thoroughly soaked afterwards‚ÄĒCat heard her voice raised in annoyance at someone who had come in to take the dull tea away and caught her with her trunk open. When Gwendolen finally unlocked the bathroom door, she was still angry.

‚ÄúI don‚Äôt think the servants here are very civil,‚ÄĚ she said. ‚ÄúIf that girl says one thing more, she‚Äôll find herself with a boil on her nose‚ÄĒeven if her name is Euphemia! Though,‚ÄĚ Gwendolen added charitably, ‚ÄúI‚Äôm inclined to think being called Euphemia is punishment enough for anyone. You have to go and get your new suit on, Cat. She says dinner‚Äôs in half an hour and we have to change for it. Did you ever hear anything so formal and unnatural!‚ÄĚ

‚ÄúI thought you were looking forward to that kind of thing,‚ÄĚ said Cat, who most certainly was not.

‚ÄúYou can be grand and natural,‚ÄĚ Gwendolen retorted. But the thought of the coming grandeur soothed her all the same. ‚ÄúI shall wear my blue dress with the lace collar,‚ÄĚ she said. ‚ÄúAnd I do think being called Euphemia is a heavy enough burden for anyone to bear, however rude they are.‚ÄĚ

As Cat went up his winding stair, the Castle filled with a mysterious booming. It was the first noise he had heard. It alarmed him. He learned later that it was the dressing gong, to warn the Family that they had half an hour to change in. Cat, of course, did not take nearly that time to put his suit on. So he had yet another shower. He felt damp and weak and almost washed out of existence by the time the maid who was so unfortunate in being called Euphemia came to take him and Gwendolen downstairs to the drawing room where the Family was waiting.

vocabulary.definition: Cause someone to feel frightened, disturbed, or in danger.
vocabulary.example: The sudden noise alarmed the children.

vocabulary.definition: A feeling of being irritated or bothered.
vocabulary.example: Her lateness caused him annoyance.

vocabulary.definition: A heavy load, either physical or metaphorical; a duty or responsibility that causes stress or worry.
vocabulary.example: She felt the burden of responsibility.

vocabulary.definition: In a kind or generous way, often forgiving in judging others.
vocabulary.example: He spoke charitably about his opponent‚Äôs mistakes.

vocabulary.definition: Polite and courteous in behaviour.
vocabulary.example: She was civil despite the disagreement.

vocabulary.definition: Slightly wet, often in an unpleasant way.
vocabulary.example: The walls were damp after the rain.

vocabulary.definition: Prevents someone from giving full attention to something.
vocabulary.example: Noise distracts her from studying.

vocabulary.definition: Covered or hung with cloth in loose folds.
vocabulary.example: The statue was draped with velvet cloth.

vocabulary.definition: Lacking interest or excitement; boring; lacking flavour or brightness.
vocabulary.example: The meal tasted dull and unappetizing.

vocabulary.definition: The state or fact of living or having objective reality.
vocabulary.example: He questioned the meaning of existence.

vocabulary.definition: Following established rules or customs; suitable for official occasions.
vocabulary.example: They wore formal clothes to the event.

vocabulary.definition: Decorative wreaths or chains of flowers, leaves, or other materials, typically hung or worn for decoration.
vocabulary.example: The hall was decorated with flower garlands.

vocabulary.definition: Covered thinly with gold leaf or gold paint.
vocabulary.example: The frame of the mirror was gilded.

vocabulary.definition: Splendour and impressiveness, especially of appearance or style.
vocabulary.example: The palace was full of grandeur.

vocabulary.definition: Great comfort or elegance, especially when involving great expense.
vocabulary.example: She enjoyed a life of luxury.

vocabulary.definition: A tall wooden pole decorated and used in traditional European folk festivals, around which a maypole dance takes place.
vocabulary.example: The children danced around the maypole.

vocabulary.definition: Difficult or impossible to understand, explain, or identify.
vocabulary.example: A mysterious sound came from the woods.

vocabulary.definition: A dull grey alloy of tin with copper and other metals, used for ornamental ware and utensils.
vocabulary.example: The teapot was made of pewter.

vocabulary.definition: Having the qualities or bearing of a queen; regal and dignified.
vocabulary.example: She sat with a queenly poise.

vocabulary.definition: Answered sharply or in a witty or angry way.
vocabulary.example: He retorted quickly to the criticism.

vocabulary.definition: Spoke with sudden involuntary pauses and a tendency to repeat the initial letters of words.
vocabulary.example: He stammered when asking if he could have some tea.

vocabulary.definition: Very hungry.
vocabulary.example: He felt starving after the long walk.

vocabulary.definition: Marked by or causing bad luck or regret.
vocabulary.example: It was an unfortunate mistake.

vocabulary.definition: To remove things from a suitcase, bag, or box.
vocabulary.example: She decided to unpack her belongings herself.

vocabulary.definition: Furniture provided with a soft covering of fabric and padding.
vocabulary.example: The sofa was upholstered in fine leather.

vocabulary.definition: Decorative paper used to cover the walls of a room.
vocabulary.example: The room had floral wallpaper.

vocabulary.definition: Having many turns or bends.
vocabulary.example: They walked up the winding path.
